story.title: Dr. Brooks Models a Population's Race for Survival
story.text:
I am Dr. Terrence Brooks. At Harvard University, I build computer models to see if animal populations can survive. My work helps us understand evolution in action and protect endangered species.

In my model, I can increase the environmental pressure. This could be a new disease or a hotter climate. This pressure challenges the population. A population's best defense is its population diversity. A group with many different traits has more chances to survive. High diversity makes it more likely that some animals have the right trait.

When this happens, the favorable trait frequency goes up. More individuals with this trait survive, which increases the overall survival rate. These survivors have more babies, increasing the reproduction rate. They pass their successful traits to the next generation. This is natural selection. My goal is to see if this process happens fast enough. Can the population adapt before the environment changes too much?
think_abouts: What happens to the survival rate when environmental pressure goes up?
Why is a high population diversity like having more tools in a toolbox?
story.go_deeper: Humans used selective breeding to create different dog breeds. How might this reduce their population diversity and make some breeds vulnerable to disease?

margin_defs.definition: Simple computer programs to test ideas.
To change over time to fit a new situation.

check_prompts: What process allows populations to adapt to their environment over time?

vocabulary.definition: Any factor in the environment that affects which organisms survive and reproduce.
vocabulary.use_sentence: A long drought is a strong environmental pressure on desert plants.

vocabulary.definition: The range of different traits and genetic variations within a group of organisms.
vocabulary.use_sentence: The high population diversity of the insects helped them survive the new pesticide.

vocabulary.definition: The percentage of a population that carries a trait suited to the current environment.
vocabulary.use_sentence: After the cold winter, the favorable trait frequency for thick fur increased in the wolf pack.

vocabulary.definition: The process by which organisms with traits better suited to their environment survive and reproduce more.
vocabulary.use_sentence: Through natural selection, giraffes developed long necks to reach high leaves.

vocabulary.definition: An organism's ability to survive and reproduce in its specific environment.
vocabulary.use_sentence: The rabbit's speed gives it high fitness in an area with many foxes.

discussion_cards.prompt: Florida panthers were saved by bringing in Texas cougars. Why did adding new animals with different traits (increasing diversity) help the population survive?
discussion_cards.frame: Adding new animals helped the panthers because...

discussion_cards.prompt: If a small group of birds colonizes a new island, why might they have less population diversity than the birds on the mainland?
discussion_cards.frame: The new island population might have less diversity because...

reflection_frames.knew: Before reading, I already knew that ___
reflection_frames.learned: From the story, I learned that ___
